channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: France just approved a social media ban for kids under 15
item.description: French lawmakers approved a bill banning social media for children under 15, paving the way for the measure to enter into force at the start of the next school year in September, as the idea of setting a minimum age for use of the platforms gains momentum across Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Video shows flames flying from NASA plane that touched down without landing gear
item.description: A NASA research plane has landed without its landing gear in Texas, sending plumes of flame behind it.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In Davos, warnings about AI and jobs
item.description: The impact of AI on the labor market is more serious than previously expected, with entry-level jobs collapsing and white-collar jobs in danger, participants at the World Economic Forum (WEF) said last week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon to close Amazon Go and Amazon Fresh to concentrate on Whole Foods and grocery delivery
item.description: Amazon said it's closing all of its Amazon Go and Amazon Fresh locations, as the online behemoth focuses on its grocery delivery, Whole Foods Market and a new "supersized" store concept.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Grubhub confirms data breach amid extortion claims
item.description: Grubhub data breach confirmed as sources say ShinyHunters group allegedly demands Bitcoin payment to prevent release of stolen customer support data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Following its major restructuring announcement, Ubisoft begins 'a collective, voluntary mutual termination agreement' at its Paris HQ, with 200 jobs on the line
item.description: After announcing major restructuring plans at Ubisoft and the cancellation of several games, the company has now begun a voluntary redundancy process at its Paris, France headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Demi Williams, TechRadar

item.title: European Union opens investigation into Musk’s AI chatbot Grok over sexual deepfakes
item.description: The European Union opened a formal investigation into Elon Musk's social media platform X on Monday after his artificial intelligence chatbot Grok spewed nonconsensual sexualized deepfake images on the platform.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel wrestling with CPU supply shortage
item.description: Intel said that it is facing a CPU shortage, particularly affecting the data center/server business, but that it expects the problem to peak this quarter and ease throughout the year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: Intel news and insights | AMD news and insights With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: European Union opens investigation into Musk's AI chatbot Grok over sexual deepfakes
item.description: European Union regulators have opened a formal investigation into Elon Musk's social media platform X after its AI chatbot Grok started producing nonconsensual sexualized deepfake images.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: With AI, the database matters again
item.description: Developers have spent the past decade trying to forget databases exist. Not literally, of course. We still store petabytes. But for the average developer, the database became an implementation detail; an essential but staid utility layer we worked hard not to think about.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 16 open source projects transforming AI and machine learning
item.description: For several decades now, the most innovative software has always emerged from the world of open source software. It’s no different with machine learning and large language models.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech layoffs: A 2026 timeline
item.description: Among a range of factors leading to a wave of tech sector layoffs in 2026 is the rapid rise of artificial Intelligence (AI) and automation. Companies are reconfiguring their workforces to leverage AI for increased efficiency and reduced operating costs.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why clicking the wrong Copilot link could put your data at risk
item.description: Microsoft Copilot security vulnerability allows attackers to steal data through malicious links. Learn how the attack works, and protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Winter storms can knock out your tech fast: Prepare now
item.description: Major winter storm threatens widespread power outages across the US starting Friday. Essential tech prep tips to keep devices charged and stay connected.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: National Design Studio looks to overhaul 27,000 federal websites
item.description: The Trump administration has already taken steps to eliminate about 330 websites it deems unnecessary or duplicative. The post National Design Studio looks to overhaul 27,000 federal websites — and is hiring a team to do it first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Go developers meh on AI coding tools – survey
item.description: Most Go language developers are using AI-powered software development tools, but their satisfaction with these tools is middling, according to the 2025 Go Developer Survey. The survey also found that the vast majority of Go developers—91%—were satisfied with using the language.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Historic infrastructure buildout for AI
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter gives readers the latest AI technology advancements, covering the challenges and opportunities AI presents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DCN becoming the new WAN for AI-era applications
item.description: The tried and true enterprise WAN market is undergoing a shift and consolidating around a new architecture class. AI workloads and hybrid cloud deployments are exposing the operational costs of managing separate control planes for connectivity, security, and application delivery.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Workforce, supply chain factor into reauthorizing National Quantum Initiative
item.description: House and Senate lawmakers are both advancing legislation to reauthorize and update the National Quantum Initiative. The post Workforce, supply chain factor into reauthorizing National Quantum Initiative first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel nabs Qualcomm veteran to lead GPU initiative
item.description: Intel and Qualcomm have traded executives several times over the years, and the trading continues with Intel luring away Qualcomm’s GPU lead. Eric Demers, a long-time industry veteran GPU engineer, joins Intel as senior vice president and the lead of its GPU engineering team.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ahead of filing season, IRS scraps customer service metric it’s used for 20 years
item.description: An IRS watchdog told Congress last year that the discarded metric is “misleading,” and "does not accurately reflect the experience of most taxpayers who call." The post Ahead of filing season, IRS scraps customer service metric it’s used for 20 years first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: With a new executive order clearing the path for federal AI standards, the question now is whether Congress will finish the job
item.description: "By virtue of establishing an AI Litigation Task Force, the president is trying to make sure that Congress has a clear path to move forward," Kevin Frazier said The post With a new executive order clearing the path for federal AI standards, the question now is whether Congress will finish the job fi
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network
